description: Checkmarx SAST finding patterns mapped to compliance gate rules and fix patterns. Used by compliance-gate.yml and security-vuln-scanner.yml to prevent recurring vulnerabilities.

patterns.description: User input from req.body/req.query/req.params flows to res.json() without sanitization
patterns.fixPattern: Import and apply sanitizeForOutput() to any user-derived data before including in response. For error objects, use sanitizeForOutput(msg) instead of raw error.message.
patterns.preventionCheck: Every .ts file that reads req.body/query/params AND calls res.json/send MUST import sanitizeForOutput from util/security

patterns.description: User input from req.body influences the target URL of a server-side HTTP request
patterns.fixPattern: Always validate URLs with validateTrustedUrl() before fetch(). Use resolveTrustedRegisteredAgentExecuteUrl() to build URLs from a trusted registry (never concatenate user input into URLs). Parse input with parseSafeIdentifier() to strip injection chars.

codeExample.vulnerable: const url = body.agentUrl; const resp = await fetch(url, ...)
codeExample.fixed: const url = resolveTrustedRegisteredAgentExecuteUrl({ cluster }); if (!validateTrustedUrl(url)) return; const resp = await fetch(url, ...)

patterns.preventionCheck: Every file using fetch()/postJson()/callAgent() MUST have validateTrustedUrl(). Every file resolving URLs from user input MUST use resolveTrustedRegisteredAgentExecuteUrl().

patterns.description: A loop's iteration count is influenced by user input, potentially causing denial of service
patterns.fixPattern: Add explicit Math.min(array.length, MAX_CONSTANT) to ALL for-loop bounds. For array responses, use .slice(0, MAX_ENTRIES) before iteration. Define MAX_* constants at module scope.

codeExample.fixed: const MAX_SCORE_FIELDS = 4; for (let i = 0; i < Math.min(scores.length, MAX_SCORE_FIELDS); i++) { ... }

patterns.preventionCheck: ANY for-loop with .length in the bound MUST use Math.min(x, MAX_CONSTANT). Arrays returned in responses MUST use .slice(0, MAX_ENTRIES). Compliance gate Rule 12 enforces this.
indirectFlows: req.query.uuid → getAgentExecutionLogs → getExecutionSnapshot → pickPreferredSnapshot → for-loop on currentScore.length

patterns.description: Internal error details (stack traces, error messages) exposed in HTTP responses
patterns.fixPattern: Always wrap error.message with sanitizeForOutput(msg) which strips dangerous chars and limits to 256 chars
patterns.preventionCheck: Compliance gate Rule 10 warns when error responses contain unsanitized message/detail fields

securityImports.description: Required security utility imports for controller files

sanitizeForOutput.usage: Sanitize any user-derived data before including in HTTP response
sanitizeForOutput.requiredWhen: File reads req.body/query/params AND writes res.json/send

validateTrustedUrl.usage: Validate URL against trusted domain pattern before fetch()

parseSafeIdentifier.usage: Parse and sanitize identifier strings (cluster, namespace, etc.)
parseSafeIdentifier.requiredWhen: File uses user input to resolve URLs or build identifiers

safeString.usage: Trim and limit string input from request
safeString.requiredWhen: File reads string values from req.query/params

resolveTrustedRegisteredAgentExecuteUrl.usage: Resolve agent URL from trusted registry (never build URLs from user input)
resolveTrustedRegisteredAgentExecuteUrl.requiredWhen: File needs to call an agent endpoint

complianceGateRules.rule10_xss: File with req input + res output MUST have sanitizeForOutput
complianceGateRules.rule11_ssrf: File with fetch/postJson/callAgent MUST have validateTrustedUrl. File resolving URLs MUST validate.
complianceGateRules.rule12_dos: for-loop with .length MUST use Math.min. Arrays from DB/external MUST .slice(0, MAX). forEach/map on req-derived arrays MUST have .slice bound.
